understanding.goal_understanding: The objective in each KA59 level was to end with every goal tile marked '+' occupied at the same time by a movable/selectable piece; completing all levels by achieving WIN on each one wins the whole game within the step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement was done either by directional MOVE actions or (in these levels) by CLICKing specific coordinates to command the currently-selected piece to relocate/slide to a target position when a valid path/rule allowed it; if a click was obstructed by walls/obstacles, illegal by the level’s movement rules, or the destination was unreachable, the piece would not move but the step would still be consumed. Selection could be changed by clicking directly on another selectable piece’s position, after which subsequent actions applied to that newly selected piece. Walls/blocked tiles prevented passage, and pieces could not move through them; pushing (when present) would only work if the pushed object had space to move into, otherwise the move would be blocked.

Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: The objective in each KA59 level was to end with every goal tile marked '+' occupied at the same time by a movable/selectable piece; completing all levels by achieving WIN on each one wins the whole game within the step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement was done either by directional MOVE actions or (in these levels) by CLICKing specific coordinates to command the currently-selected piece to relocate/slide to a target position when a valid path/rule allowed it; if a click was obstructed by walls/obstacles, illegal by the level’s movement rules, or the destination was unreachable, the piece would not move but the step would still be consumed. Selection could be changed by clicking directly on another selectable piece’s position, after which subsequent actions applied to that newly selected piece. Walls/blocked tiles prevented passage, and pieces could not move through them; pushing (when present) would only work if the pushed object had space to move into, otherwise the move would be blocked.
